stem: A factory produces textile rolls using two sources. Loom Section East handles 76% of production with a known defect rate of 1.0%. Loom Section West handles 24% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 6.6% and 11.3%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected textile roll is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Loom Section West?

ground_truth.answer: P(Loom Section West | defective) ranges from 0.676 to 0.781 as Loom Section West's defect rate varies from 6.6% to 11.3%. Point estimate at 8.9%: 0.738.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.676, 0.781] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
